turns.content:
Research the latest developments in quantum computing

Note: Do NOT use these tools: [object Object]

🔍 ANALYSIS: Significant momentum across hardware, error correction, industry consolidation, and commercial deployments in early 2026.

⚡ ACTIONS: Searched multiple sources for the latest quantum computing developments.

✅ RESULTS: Compiled key findings below.

📊 STATUS: Research complete.

📖 STORY EXPLANATION:
1. **Error-corrected quantum computers shipping** — Microsoft + Atom Computing delivering level-two error-corrected systems to Denmark's Novo Nordisk Foundation. QuEra shipped one to Japan's AIST and plans global availability in 2026.
2. **D-Wave's scalability breakthrough** — Demonstrated industry-first on-chip cryogenic control for gate-model qubits, solving a major barrier to commercially viable quantum hardware.
3. **D-Wave acquires Quantum Circuits** — $550M merger to bring superconducting gate-model systems to market, positioning D-Wave as the largest quantum computing company.
4. **Neutral-atom systems scaling fast** — Arrays now exceed 6,100 atoms with 99.98% single-qubit accuracy, making neutral atoms the breakout platform of 2026.
5. **Stanford's million-qubit pathway** — Miniature optical cavities that read many qubits simultaneously; 500-cavity prototypes already built, with a realistic route to million-qubit networks.
6. **IBM Quantum Loon processor** — Experimental chip validating a new architecture for practical, high-efficiency fault-tolerant quantum error correction.
7. **Quantum security vulnerabilities found** — Researchers warn that quantum computers themselves have serious security flaws that need addressing before widespread deployment.
8. **Government investing heavily** — Los Alamos launched a Center for Quantum Computing; Infleqtion secured $6.2M ARPA-E contract to optimize the US power grid with quantum; adoption still early (only 13% of orgs have quantum in production).

⭐ RATE (1-10):

🗣️ Kaya: Error-corrected quantum computers now shipping commercially. D-Wave, Microsoft, and neutral-atom platforms leading 2026 breakthroughs.

---

Sources:
- [D-Wave's First Major Breakthrough of 2026](https://www.fastcompany.com/91469364/d-wave-quantum-computing-first-major-breakthrough-of-2026-scalable-technology)
- [Neutral Atom Quantum Computing: 2026's Big Leap — IEEE Spectrum](https://spectrum.ieee.org/neutral-atom-quantum-computing)
- [Stanford's Tiny Light Trap for Million-Qubit Computers](https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2026/02/260201223737.htm)
- [D-Wave Acquires Quantum Circuits Inc.](https://ir.dwavequantum.com/news/news-details/2026/D-Wave-Announces-Agreement-to-Acquire-Quantum-Circuits-Inc--Establishing-Worlds-Leading-Quantum-Computing-Company/default.aspx)
- [Quantum Computers Have Serious Security Flaws](https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2026/01/260120000330.htm)
- [Los Alamos Quantum Computing Research Center](https://thequantuminsider.com/2026/02/06/los-alamos-forms-quantum-computing-focused-research-center/)
- [IBM Quantum Processors & Algorithm Breakthroughs](https://newsroom.ibm.com/2025-11-12-ibm-delivers-new-quantum-processors,-software,-and-algorithm-breakthroughs-on-path-to-advantage-and-fault-tolerance)
- [7 Quantum Trends Shaping 2026 — Bernard Marr](https://bernardmarr.com/7-quantum-computing-trends-that-will-shape-every-industry-in-2026/)
- [GQI Top Predictions for 2026](https://quantumcomputingreport.com/gqis-top-predictions-for-quantum-technology-in-2026/)
- [Quantum's Transistor Moment](https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2026/01/260127010136.htm)
